etymology.description: 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + 辛(辛) (xīn) combination -> phonetic development possibly mixed with 舌(舌) (shé, "tongue") -> reference to speech
etymology.interpretation: The character 言 (yán, "speech") originates from depicting a mouth 口 (kǒu, "mouth") in combination with another form, possibly 永 or 辛, symbolizing the action or place of speaking. Over time, it may have involved 舌(舌) (shé, "tongue") to reinforce its association with speech. This combination intuitively leads to the concept of 'speech' as it directly relates to vocal articulation and communication.
etymology.simplification: 讠(言): Simplified by adopting a single-stroke variant from cursive script of 訁, reducing strokes dramatically to represent speech.

examples.chinese: 在讨论会上(在討論會上)，他的发言(他的發言)很有深度(很有深度)。
examples.pinyin: Zài tǎolùn huì shàng, tā de fāyán hěn yǒu shēndù.
examples.english: In the discussion meeting, his speech was very profound.

examples.chinese: 他总是用简洁的言词(他總是用簡潔的言詞)表达想法(表達想法)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒngshì yòng jiǎn jié de yán cí biǎodá xiǎngfǎ.
examples.english: He always uses concise speech to express his ideas.

examples.chinese: 他们的辩论(他們的辯論)十分激烈(十分激烈)，双方的言辞(雙方的言辭)都很犀利(都很犀利)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen de biànlùn shífēn jīliè, shuāngfāng de yáncí dōu hěn xīlì.
examples.english: Their debate was very intense, and the remarks from both sides were sharp.
